chapter_title.en: Chapter 72 – Al-Zahaar (likening the wife as a mother)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/256)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/256)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنِّي قُلْتُ لِامْرَأَتِي أَنْتِ عَلَيَّ كَظَهْرِ أُمِّي إِنْ خَرَجْتِ مِنْ بَابِ الْحُجْرَةِ فَخَرَجَتْ فَقَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ شَيْ‏ءٌ فَقُلْتُ إِنِّي قَوِيٌّ عَلَى أَنْ أُكَفِّرَ فَقَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ شَيْ‏ءٌ قُلْتُ إِنِّي قَوِيٌّ عَلَى أَنْ أُكَفِّرَ رَقَبَةً وَ رَقَبَتَيْنِ قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ شَيْ‏ءٌ قَوِيتَ أَوْ لَمْ تَقْوَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ibn Bukeyr, from a man from our companions, from a man who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘I said to my wife, ‘You would be unto me like the back of my mother if you go out from the door of the chamber’. So she went out. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon you’. So I said, ‘I am (financially) strong enough to expiate’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon you’. I said, ‘I am strong enough to expiate by freeing a neck and two necks’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon you, whether you are (financially) strong or not strong’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from a man of our people from a man who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about my saying to my wife, ‘You are like the back of my mother if you went out of the room’ and she went out of the room. He (the Imam) said, ‘There it does not have any valid effect on you.’ I then said, ‘I am strong to expiate.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘You do not owe anything because of it.’ I said, ‘I am strong to set free one or two slaves.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘You do not owe anything because of it, regardless you are strong or not strong.’”
